perturbation.original_text: (c) "Affiliate(s)" shall mean an entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a party. "Control," for purposes of this definition, means direct or indirect ownership or control of more than 50% of the voting interests of the subject entity.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) "Affiliate(s)" shall mean an entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a party. "Control," for purposes of this definition, means direct or indirect ownership or control of more than 25% of the voting interests of the subject entity.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 1(c) changes the definition of 'Control' from 'more than 50% of the voting interests' to 'more than 25% of the voting interests.' This creates an in-text contradiction because other sections of the contract may rely on the original 50% threshold for determining rights, obligations, or restrictions related to Affiliates. For example, any clause determining liability on the Affiliate should be only if controlling interest is of 'more than 50%', now will be subject to 25%, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall defend, indemnify and hold the other party and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents, successors, assigns, licensees and distributors harmless from and against any and all judgments, settlements, damages, penalties, costs and expenses (including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of any third party claims relating to the Indemnifying Party's breach or alleged breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the definition of 'Control' from 'more than 50% of the voting interests' to 'more than 25% of the voting interests'. The contradicted text refers to 'Affiliates' in the context of indemnification. If 'Control' is now defined as 'more than 25%', the scope of 'Affiliates' widens, potentially exposing the indemnifying party to liabilities for entities they would not have been responsible for under the original 50% definition. This creates a direct contradiction because the level of control required for indemnification is altered, impacting the obligations of each party. The original text meant control was of 'more than 50%', now with the changed text it's 'more than 25%', creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date listed above and continue for twenty (20) years, unless sooner terminated as provided in Section 7(b) [Term and Termination].
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date listed above and continue for ten (10) years, unless sooner terminated as provided in Section 7(b) [Term and Termination].
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 7(a) reduces the term of the agreement from twenty (20) years to ten (10) years. This contradicts any sections that refer to the original twenty (20)-year term or calculate obligations or benefits based on that duration. This change affects any sections defining rights or payments calculated for 20 year period, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Sections 2(a) [License Grant], 2(b) [Sublicensing], 2(c) [Display of Titles], 2(d) [Removal of Titles], 3, and 11 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement: (i) in perpetuity with respect to Titles for which the licenses granted in Section 2(a) [License Grant] are perpetual; and (ii) for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A with respect to Titles for which the license term specified in Schedule A extends beyond the expiration or termination of this Agreement. Sections 1, 7, 8(a) [Privacy and Data Collection; Reports], 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 and 17 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The modification in Section 7(a) reduces the agreement term from 20 years to 10 years. The contradicted text states that certain sections, including 2(a), 2(b), 2(c), 2(d), 3, and 11, survive 'for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A.' If Schedule A outlines license terms based on the original 20-year agreement, the changed text creating a 10-year term directly contradicts the survival clause's intention and calculation basis tied to the original duration, thereby creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement. The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Amended and Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of December 21, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.changed_text: No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement for the first five years. Thereafter, Licensee shall pay Licensor a royalty fee of 5% of the gross revenue generated from the Titles. The initial consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Amended and Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of December 21, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.explanation: The modified Section 10 introduces a royalty fee of 5% of gross revenue after the initial five years, contradicting the original clause stating that 'No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement'. This in-text contradiction creates legal uncertainty regarding the payment terms and affects the consideration clause by changing it after a period of time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6: 1) For Titles listed in Schedule A1-A2: Licensor can only grant Licensee distribution rights to up to six (6) MSOs plus two (2) of China's Internet TV license holders or their OTT Internet-based video partners by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD). China's current Internet TV license holders include: CNTV (中国网络电视台/未来电视), BesTV (百视通), Wasu (华数), Southern Media Cooperation (南方传媒), Hunan TV (芒果 TV), China National Radio/Galaxy Internet TV (GITV) (银河电视), and China Radio International (中国国际 广播电台); 2 Source: IDEANOMICS, INC., 10-K, 3/30/2016 2) For Titles listed in Schedule A3: For "天下女人": No satellite TV broadcasting rights granted. Titles other than "猜 猜女人心", "赏深越慕" , "职场新女性挑战行动", " 杨澜访谈录" (自第192期及以后的节 目),"人生相对论 ", " 天下女人", and "正青春" cannot be edited in any way; 3) For Titles listed in Schedule A4: Other than the versions (dubbed or translated) in which the Titles in Schedule A4 already exist in (and were delivered in), no further dubbing, translation, or editing may be performed by any party on Schedule A4's Titles. 4) For Titles listed in Schedule A5 (except "Summer in February"): Limitations on Rights Granted: the following distribution methods are permitted: non-theatrical, public video, ship and hotel rights (all not earlier than the US theatrical release); home video rental, home video sell through, and commercial video rights (all not earlier than the US video release); cable TV (pay & free), terrestrial TV (pay & free), satellite pay TV; internet TV (pay & free), residential and non-residential pay-per-view, residential and non-residential internet pay-per-view, VOD, near VOD, and internet VOD (all no earlier than the corresponding exploitation in the USA). Contents are dubbed and subtitled in Cantonese and Mandarin languages. Exploitation of the Title over the internet must adhere to the following: 1) Distribution is limited to the dubbed or subtitled version, provided the subtitles shall be burnt in with no option to remove them, 2) Licensor must be notified prior to the first exhibition of the Title over the internet, and 3) Authorization to use excerpts, stills, trailers and soundtrack parts of the Title for the 3 promotion of the Title only on Licensor's or Licensor's authorized agent's web site online. Source: IDEANOMICS, INC., 10-K, 3/30/2016 Rights granted for "Summer in February", shall adhere to Section 2, not subject to the limitations in section 2(a)(i)(4).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a royalty fee after five years, directly contradicting the contradicted text stating a 'royalty-free' license. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding payment terms, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
